Interface TemperatureLogRepository

All Superinterfaces:
org.springframework.data.repository.CrudRepository<TemperatureLog, Long>, org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository<TemperatureLog, Long>, org.springframework.data.repository.ListCrudRepository<TemperatureLog, Long>, org.springframework.data.repository.ListPagingAndSortingRepository<TemperatureLog, Long>, org.springframework.data.repository.PagingAndSortingRepository<TemperatureLog, Long>, org.springframework.data.repository.query.QueryByExampleExecutor<TemperatureLog>, org.springframework.data.repository.Repository<TemperatureLog, Long>
@Repository public interface TemperatureLogRepository extends org.springframework.data.jpa.repository.JpaRepository<TemperatureLog, Long>
Repository for TemperatureLog entities.

Provides queries for retrieving and counting temperature log entries scoped to an organization, used in the IK-Mat food safety compliance module. Includes support for deviation filtering and dashboard summary generation.

    Counts temperature deviation entries for a given organization within a timestamp range.

    Counts all temperature logs for a given organization within a timestamp range.

    Checks whether any temperature log has been recorded by the given user.

  • Method Details

    • findAllByEquipmentOrganizationId

      List<TemperatureLog> findAllByEquipmentOrganizationId(Long organizationId)
      Returns all temperature logs for equipment belonging to a specific organization.
      Parameters:
      organizationId - the ID of the organization
      Returns:
      a list of all temperature logs for the organization

    • findAllByEquipmentOrganizationIdAndTimestampBetween

      List<TemperatureLog> findAllByEquipmentOrganizationIdAndTimestampBetween(Long organizationId, LocalDateTime from, LocalDateTime to)
      Returns all temperature logs for a given organization within a timestamp range. Used for filtered history views and report generation.
      Parameters:
      organizationId - the ID of the organization
      from - the start of the time range (inclusive)
      to - the end of the time range (inclusive)
      Returns:
      a list of temperature logs within the given period

    • findTop10ByEquipmentOrganizationIdOrderByTimestampDesc

      List<TemperatureLog> findTop10ByEquipmentOrganizationIdOrderByTimestampDesc(Long organizationId)
      Returns the 10 most recent temperature logs for a given organization. Used for the latest-readings view in the temperature dashboard.
      Parameters:
      organizationId - the ID of the organization
      Returns:
      a list of up to 10 recent temperature logs

    • findTop5ByEquipmentOrganizationIdAndIsDeviationTrueOrderByTimestampDesc

      List<TemperatureLog> findTop5ByEquipmentOrganizationIdAndIsDeviationTrueOrderByTimestampDesc(Long organizationId)
      Returns the 5 most recent temperature deviation entries for a given organization. Used by the dashboard to highlight recent out-of-range readings.
      Parameters:
      organizationId - the ID of the organization
      Returns:
      a list of up to 5 recent temperature logs flagged as deviations

    • countByEquipmentOrganizationIdAndTimestampBetween

      long countByEquipmentOrganizationIdAndTimestampBetween(Long organizationId, LocalDateTime start, LocalDateTime end)
      Counts all temperature logs for a given organization within a timestamp range.
      Parameters:
      organizationId - the ID of the organization
      start - the start of the time range (inclusive)
      end - the end of the time range (inclusive)
      Returns:
      the total number of temperature logs in the period

    • countByEquipmentOrganizationIdAndIsDeviationTrueAndTimestampBetween

      long countByEquipmentOrganizationIdAndIsDeviationTrueAndTimestampBetween(Long organizationId, LocalDateTime start, LocalDateTime end)
      Counts temperature deviation entries for a given organization within a timestamp range. Used for generating summary statistics in the dashboard and reports.
      Parameters:
      organizationId - the ID of the organization
      start - the start of the time range (inclusive)
      end - the end of the time range (inclusive)
      Returns:
      the number of temperature logs flagged as deviations in the period

    • existsByLoggedBy

      boolean existsByLoggedBy(User user)
      Checks whether any temperature log has been recorded by the given user. Used to enforce referential integrity before deleting a user.
      Parameters:
      user - the user to check
      Returns:
      true if the user has recorded at least one temperature log
